name: Protecting Controlled Unclassified Information in Nonfederal Systems and Organizations
owner: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)
description: NIST SP 800-171 establishes the security requirements for protecting Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) when residing in nonfederal systems and organizations. Cited by DFARS 252.204-7012 for defense contractors and increasingly by other federal procurement clauses.
relationToCloudSecurity: Cloud providers serving federal contractors handling CUI must support 800-171 compliance. Many cloud regions and configurations are marketed as 'CMMC ready' / '800-171 compliant'.
relationToAISecurity: AI/ML systems processing CUI for federal contractors fall under 800-171 requirements.

license.notes: Work of NIST is in the US public domain (17 USC §105).

lifecycle.notes: Rev 3 published May 2024 (supersedes Rev 2 from 2020). Companion assessment-procedures publication: SP 800-171A.

current_extraction.notes: Source PDF from NIST CSRC, marker-extracted to markdown. extract_800_171.py parses the r3 requirements into structured CSV/JSON: 128 total entries (95 active + 33 marked withdrawn) across all 17 SP 800-53-aligned families (03.01 Access Control through 03.17 Supply Chain Risk Management).

notes: Metadata stub; acquire from NIST CSRC and extract.
